name: Gotarrendura and El Oso Loop
name_original: La Moraña abulense — circular desde Rioacabado
description: Hike the difficult 14.5-mile La Moraña abulense circular route from Rioacabado, exploring sandy plains, Mudejar architecture, and diverse bi
region_intro: Hiking around Riocabado is characterized by its agricultural plains, historical sites, and tranquil lagoons. The terrain typically features gentle undulations, with routes often circling archaeological areas or natural water bodies. This region offers a blend of open landscapes and cultural points of interest, providing accessible outdoor experiences. Best hiking trails around Riocabado The most popular hiking route is Castro de Las Cogotas from Cardeñosa — circular by the Embalse de las Cogotas, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Embalse de las Cogotas reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa, a moderate 10.1 miles (16.2 km) path. This trail explores the area around the Yacimiento de las Cogotas archaeological site. Local hikers also love the La Moraña abulense — circular desde Rioacabado, a 14.5 miles (23.4 km) trail leading through the open plains of La Moraña, often completed in about 6 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Riocabado is defined by agricultural landscapes, historical sites, and lagoons, with routes offering options for various ability levels from easy to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 4 hikers have used komoot to explore Riocabado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Riocabado?
A: There are over 14 hiking trails around Riocabado, offering a variety of experiences. These include 1 easy route, 11 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Riocabado?
A: Hiking around Riocabado is characterized by its agricultural plains and gentle undulations. Routes often explore open landscapes, historical sites, and tranquil lagoons, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Riocabado?
A: Yes, Riocabado offers several circular routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-from-cardenosa-circular-by-the-embalse-de-las-cogotas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Cogotas from Cardeñosa — circular by the Embalse de las Cogotas&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the reservoir, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137612105/yacimiento-de-las-cogotas-circular-desde-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt;, which explores an archaeological site.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking in Riocabado?
A: While hiking, you can discover several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137612105/yacimiento-de-las-cogotas-circular-desde-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt; trail explores an archaeological site. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3704019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Narros de Saldueña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5987624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro Apóstol&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature water bodies like lagoons or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes in Riocabado incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-from-cardenosa-circular-by-the-embalse-de-las-cogotas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Cogotas from Cardeñosa — circular by the Embalse de las Cogotas&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Embalse de las Cogotas reservoir. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249108582/lagunas-de-la-morana-circular-desde-el-oso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de La Moraña — circular desde El Oso&lt;/a&gt; trail specifically explores the tranquil lagoons of La Moraña.

Q: What is a good moderate hike for someone looking for a scenic experience?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-from-cardenosa-circular-by-the-embalse-de-las-cogotas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Cogotas from Cardeñosa — circular by the Embalse de las Cogotas&lt;/a&gt;. This 7.4-mile (11.9 km) trail takes about 3 hours and 14 minutes to complete and offers pleasant views of the Embalse de las Cogotas reservoir.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the Riocabado area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250106622/la-morana-abulense-circular-desde-rioacabado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Moraña abulense — circular desde Rioacabado&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.5-mile (23.4 km) trail. It leads through the open plains of La Moraña and typically takes around 6 hours and 9 minutes to complete.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Riocabado?
A: More than 4 hikers have used komoot to explore Riocabado's varied terrain. They often appreciate the blend of open landscapes and cultural points of interest, as well as the accessible outdoor experiences the region provides.

Q: Can I find trails that explore historical or archaeological sites?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137612105/yacimiento-de-las-cogotas-circular-desde-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.1-mile (16.2 km) path that specifically explores the area around the Yacimiento de las Cogotas archaeological site.

Q: Are there any shorter, moderate routes suitable for a half-day hike?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325265539/arco-de-conejeros-from-cardenosa-circular-via-the-calvario-de-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de Conejeros from Cardeñosa — circular via the Calvario de Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.7 km (4.8 miles) trail that can be completed in approximately 2 hours and 6 minutes, making it ideal for a half-day outing.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Riocabado?
A: The hikes around Riocabado offer a range of difficulties. While there is 1 easy route, the majority are moderate (11 routes), and there are also 2 difficult routes for more experienced hikers. This variety ensures options for all ability levels.
tips: Half a century ago, the Avila town of Las Berlanas suffered a catastrophe of hitherto unknown proportions. A flood devastated the municipality and one of its main surroundings, El Burgo, in addition to ending the lives of two people. To commemorate this tragic event, a monument has been erected that remembers the victims and the catastrophe, but also the efforts of the people of Berlin to improve themselves.
Mural at the entrance to the town of Gotarrendura that claims the birth of Santa Teresa in this town in Avila.

It is the parish church of Gotarrendura and its construction dates from the s. XVII.
Regarding its artistic style, it connects very well with the Mudejar style typical of its area, although its baroque part stands out more.

Opened in 2017, the center offers a variety of educational and recreational activities, including guided tours, real-time bird watching via video cameras, and exhibits on local fauna and flora.
The center also features dioramas, a screening room, and an observation deck, providing an immersive experience for visitors.

The Church of San Pedro Apóstol in El Oso, Ávila, is a parish church dating back to the 16th century. It stands out for its stone architecture, with three naves separated by large semicircular arches and a 17th-century Baroque main altarpiece. ⛪😎
In front of the church, there is the Stone Bear, also known as Verraco Vettón. This stone sculpture is part of the town's Open Air Museum and has great historical and cultural importance.🐻

The museum allows visitors to explore the cultural and natural heritage of El Oso while enjoying a walk in the open air. It is a great way to learn more about the history and traditions of this small municipality in Ávila. 🌄😎🚶‍♂️

faqs.question: Is this circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this specific circular route from Rioacabado is classified as difficult hiking. While the broader La Moraña region has many easy, flat trails, this particular route requires a higher level of fitness and experience. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses the vast, flat, and undulating plains of La Moraña. You'll encounter sandy soils rich in quartz and feldspar, with small elevations and depressions indicating ancient, fossilized dunes. The landscape is primarily composed of extensive cereal fields, interspersed with pine forests and oak groves. Along riverbanks, you might find small willows and bushes.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a blend of natural beauty and historical architecture. The plains offer wide-open skies and impressive sunsets, often referred to as the 'Sea of La Moraña'. You'll also see numerous Mudejar-style churches and hermitages, reflecting the region's rich heritage. Pine forests add diversity, and the area is important for birdwatching, with steppe and aquatic birds.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several notable points of interest, including the Commemorative Monument of the Las Berlanas Flood, the Mural of the Birth of Santa Teresa de Ahumado, and the Church of San Miguel Arcángel of Gotarrendura. You'll also find the Interpretation Center of the Lagunas de la Moraña, the Church of San Pedro Apóstol and the Verraco Vettón (Stone Bear), and the Tree of Life of El Oso.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular hike?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the Rioacabado trailhead are not provided, Rioacabado is a small village. You can typically find parking in or near the village center, or at designated spots close to the start of the trail. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Ruta Teresiana, Ruta del Sureste, and the Camino de Santiago del Sureste. Other smaller paths like Camino de Gotarrendura and Camino del Cirujano Gallego also cross the route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region. However, given the presence of agricultural fields and potential wildlife, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local regulations and private property. Be mindful of livestock if present.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike in La Moraña. The weather is milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot with little shade, and winters can be cold and potentially wet. Consider the open nature of the plains when planning your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular route from Rioacabado. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Rioacabado is a small village, so amenities might be limited. For more options like cafes, pubs, and accommodation, you might need to visit larger nearby towns such as Arévalo. It's a good idea to bring your own water and snacks for the duration of the hike.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The region is known for its bird diversity, especially steppe birds like bustards, sandgrouse, and Montagu's harriers. You might also spot various aquatic birds near riverbanks and lagoons. The pine forests also host a variety of flora and fauna.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' classification and varied terrain, wear sturdy hiking boots. Layers of clothing are recommended, as conditions can change. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. A first-aid kit is also advisable.
